title: English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26
summary: The checked-in CME00104 XML is a repository copy of the University of Michigan CME encoding of printed pages 86-115 of Frederick Morgan Padelford's 1912 Anglia article, not an encoding of the article's introductory pages 79-85. Those encoded pages comprise Padelford's thirty-one numbered English-song transcriptions from Bodleian MS Arch. Selden B. 26, distributed across current folios 5r-33v, together with interleaved variants and comparisons. Padelford transcribed the Selden texts directly, used four collateral manuscript witnesses directly, followed Fuller Maitland's 1891 edition for six Trinity Carol Roll versions, and drew three comparisons from Julius Zupitza's 1892 edition of Cambridge Ee.1.12. A complete Selden IIIF bundle proves that song 31 is the later poem on f. 33v, while DIMEV shows that Padelford omitted a separate four-line marginal poem on f. 23r. Literal Padelford references to Archiv volume 82 page 206 and Cambridge Ee.1.2 are retained as citation evidence but do not match the located source: the corresponding texts occur in Zupitza's volume 89 edition from Ee.1.12.
review_notes: The preferred subject is Padelford's selected English songs plus the apparatus retained on printed pages 86-115, not the complete Anglia article, the complete Selden Carol Book music component, or the composite codex.
The source-song boundary is current folios 5r-33v / IIIF items 11-68. The preliminary 5r-33r boundary would omit numbered song 31.
The physical leaf range includes intervening Latin musical compositions that Padelford did not select; the thirty-one songs are distributed through the range rather than exhausting its contents.
Rights and access statements are entity-specific. In particular, medieval text, historical edition, digital encoding, and modern manuscript-image rights must not be conflated.
Negative public-copy findings are dated 2026-07-13 and do not prove that no private, licensed, or unindexed copy exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00104
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00104; TEXT/@LANG is enm.
The source submodule is distinct from these manifests and is not modified by this record.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00104
entities.description: A publicly accessible keyed and structurally marked-up text of the transcriptions and apparatus on Padelford's printed pages 86-115.
notes: The web text begins with printed page 86 and ends on page 115; Padelford's title and introductory discussion on pages 79-85 are bibliographic and lineage evidence but are not encoded in this item.

entities.label: Padelford 1912 edition

date_statements.label: article completion
date_statements.notes: The dateline at the article's close records completion, not publication.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick Morgan Padelford, ‘English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26,’ Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 36 (N.F. 24) (1912): 79-115.
bibliographic.title: English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26

notes: The complete article occupies printed pages 79-115; CME00104 represents only pages 86-115.

Padelford's pages 80-85 discuss hands and dating. His claim that the orthography is not earlier than about 1570-1575 is retained as a historical editorial opinion and is not adopted as the cataloged work date.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Arch. Selden B. 26
entities.description: A composite manuscript of five originally separate English manuscripts. The relevant music component is folios 3-33; Padelford's thirty-one selected English songs begin at current f. 5r and end with a later addition at f. 33v.

date_statements.label: music component
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century; DIAMM refines the principal copying to 1425-1440 and about 1450

physical_description.support: Parchment for the Selden Carol Book component
physical_description.extent: Composite codex; relevant music component folios 3-33
notes: DIAMM inventories 53 musical compositions in the component, including Latin and English pieces.
DIMEV records 32 English verse witnesses in the manuscript, one more than Padelford's numbered selection.
The first selected English song begins at f. 5r; f. 4v contains Latin material.
Song 30 is on f. 33r and song 31, ‘Yf thow fle idelnes,’ is on f. 33v. The next recto begins unrelated Gregory material.
A separate faint four-line poem beginning ‘Hail blessed Lady’ appears in the lower margin of f. 23r. DIMEV 1699 catalogs it; Padelford and CME00104 omit it.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of MS. Arch. Selden B. 26
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and JPEG derivatives
notes: The published object has 274 canvases. Cached item 11 is f. 5r, item 67 is f. 33r, and item 68 is f. 33v.

entities.label: Early Bodleian Music
entities.description: Sir John Stainer's two-volume 1901 edition, with J. F. R. Stainer and C. Stainer's transcriptions and a first volume of facsimiles. Padelford says all the Selden songs had already appeared there and cites a reading for song 24.

bibliographic.citation: John Stainer, ed., Sacred and Secular Songs together with other MS. Compositions in the Bodleian Library, Oxford, Ranging from about A.D. 1185 to about A.D. 1505, 2 vols. (London: Novello, Ewer and Co., 1901).
bibliographic.title: Sacred and Secular Songs together with other MS. Compositions in the Bodleian Library, Oxford

entities.label: British Library Add MS 5665
entities.description: A late-fifteenth- to early-sixteenth-century collection of church services, hymns, carols, and songs. Padelford directly gives collateral readings from historical folios 4b, 28b, 31b, and 42b.
notes: No Digitised Content link is exposed in the current interim British Library catalog record.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. e. 1
entities.description: A collection of religious and other songs and carols dated about 1460-1480. Padelford cites historical folios 24a and 32b, normalized here as current 24r and 32v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian single-slide object for MS. Eng. poet. e. 1
entities.description: The provider object exposes only f. 41v, not either Padelford target at f. 24r or f. 32v.

entities.label: Balliol College MS 354
entities.description: Richard Hill's paper commonplace-book, compiled in London between 1501 and 1533. Padelford supplies collateral song versions from historical folios 220b and 222a, corresponding to current 220v and 222r.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF facsimile of Balliol MS 354
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and JPEG derivatives
notes: The 528-canvas provider object is cached completely. The targets are provider p. 444 / cached item 451 for f. 220v and provider p. 447 / cached item 454 for f. 222r.

entities.label: British Library Sloane MS 2593
entities.description: A fourteenth- and fifteenth-century poetry collection. Padelford gives collateral readings from historical folios 25a and 30b, normalized here as 25r and 30v.
notes: The current catalog exposes no public manuscript images; physical consultation is onsite.

entities.label: English Carols of the Fifteenth Century
entities.description: Fuller Maitland's edition of the Trinity Carol Roll. Padelford says his Trinity readings follow this edition except that he transcribed the Agincourt song from its frontispiece.

bibliographic.citation: J. A. Fuller Maitland, ed., English Carols of the Fifteenth Century from a MS. Roll in the Library of Trinity College, Cambridge, with added vocal parts by W. S. Rockstro (London: Leadenhall Press, 1891).
bibliographic.title: English Carols of the Fifteenth Century from a MS. Roll in the Library of Trinity College, Cambridge

notes: The cached public scan is partial: it includes the frontispiece, preliminaries, and original notation/text through printed page 27, enough to verify Padelford's six used items, but omits later modern notation and appendix pages.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.3.58
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment roll containing thirteen Latin and Middle English carols with music. Padelford's apparatus uses Fuller Maitland carols I, II, III, VII, IX, and X.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge complete IIIF facsimile of MS O.3.58
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and JPEG derivatives
notes: The provider object exposes sixteen canvases and is cached completely.

entities.label: Zupitza 1892 edition of Jacob Ryman's poems

bibliographic.title: Die Gedichte des Franziskaners Jakob Ryman
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: The Padelford comparison texts occur at volume 89 pages 204, 206, and 238.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ee.1.12
entities.description: The manuscript source identified by Zupitza for his edition of Jacob Ryman's poems. Padelford's song 11 citation is consistent with this shelfmark; the song 4 citation prints Ee.1.2, probably omitting the final 1.

holding.notes: Current public facsimile access and an exact current catalog record were not verified in this review.

entities.label: Archiv volume 82, Padelford's literal song 10 citation target
entities.description: The 1889 Archiv volume literally cited by Padelford as ‘Archiv 82.206.’ Its page 206 contains unrelated manuscript-legends material, not the song comparison; the matching text is Zupitza's volume 89 page 206.

bibliographic.citation: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 82 (1889), p. 206, literal citation target in Padelford.

entities.description: A prior printed anthology in which Padelford identifies six of his selected songs as already published: songs 4, 10, 15, 28, 29, and 30.

entities.label: DIMEV 1.0 records for MS. Arch. Selden B. 26
entities.description: The exact DIMEV 1.0 release catalogs 32 English verse witnesses in the manuscript. Thirty-one map to Padelford's numbered source songs; DIMEV 1699 is an omitted marginal quatrain on f. 23r, and DIMEV 2403 places song 31 on f. 33v.

editorial_practices.description: Padelford numbers and transcribes thirty-one English songs from the Selden component. The selection excludes intervening Latin musical items and the independent marginal English quatrain on f. 23r; it is therefore neither all 53 DIAMM musical compositions nor all 32 DIMEV English verse records.

Padelford furnishes variants known to him. He says the variants are from his own transcriptions except the Cambridge O.3.58 readings, which follow Fuller Maitland apart from his transcription of the Agincourt frontispiece. His apparatus also quotes versions from Add MS 5665, Eng. poet. e. 1, Balliol 354, Sloane 2593, and Zupitza's Ryman edition.
editorial_practices.scope: Interleaved apparatus to the thirty-one numbered songs

editorial_practices.description: The encoding begins with printed page 86 and includes songs 1-31 plus their apparatus through page 115; it omits the article title, introduction, manuscript-hand and dating discussion, and first-line index on pages 79-85.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00104.xml.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for identifiers, encoded pagination, hierarchy, text, and apparatus.

evidence.citation: Namespace-aware computational structure audit of CME00104.xml.
evidence.summary: Counts 1 DIV1, 31 DIV2, 12 DIV3, 31 HEAD, 254 LG, 987 L, 26 P, 30 PB, and 159 MILESTONE elements. Direct song divisions contain 186 LG/717 L; apparatus DIV3 contains 68 LG/266 L; four further L elements occur in prose comparison apparatus.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00104.
evidence.locator: bibliographic header; web text pp. 86-115; rights statement
evidence.summary: Official item page supplies Padelford's title and responsibility, 1912 publication facts, stable identifier, web text boundary, and No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection description.
evidence.summary: Describes the collection's public-domain source editions, manual keying, proofing, editing, and basic TEI-style structural markup.

evidence.citation: Frederick Morgan Padelford, ‘English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26,’ Anglia 36 (N.F. 24) (1912), 79-115, in complete volume scan.
evidence.summary: Complete immediate-edition evidence for title, publication, article boundary, thirty-one transcriptions, apparatus, citations, and closing dateline and signature.

evidence.citation: Padelford 1912, introductory source and collation statements.
evidence.summary: States the thirty-one-song Selden selection, prior Stainer and Chambers-Sidgwick publications, Padelford's direct-transcription policy, the Fuller Maitland exception for Trinity O.3.58, historical hand/date arguments, and a thirty-one-item first-line index.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for MS. Arch. Selden B. 26.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, composite structure, music-component description, dates, languages, holding, image attribution/license, and complete public facsimile sequence.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of cached Digital Bodleian MS. Arch. Selden B. 26 images against Padelford and CME00104.
evidence.locator: items 11, 47, 67, 68 and adjacent leaves; current ff. 5r, 23r, 33r, 33v
evidence.summary: Confirms song 1 at f. 5r, song 30 at f. 33r, song 31 at f. 33v, the independent lower-margin poem at f. 23r, and unrelated material after the target boundary.

evidence.citation: Digital Image Archive of Medieval Music, GB-Ob MS Arch. Selden. B. 26, source 803.
evidence.locator: source description, dates, provenance, and 53-item musical inventory
evidence.summary: Describes the music component as 31 parchment folios with 53 compositions; gives 1425-1440/about-1450 dates and a later f. 33v addition about 1500; proposes Worcester Abbey provenance. Its inventory ends at f. 33r and therefore does not inventory the later nonmusical f. 33v poem.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, Zenodo record 14585961, manuscript key BodArcSelB26.
evidence.locator: MSSIndex.xml BodArcSelB26; Records.xml 32 linked witnesses; records 1699 and 2403
evidence.summary: Exact CC BY 4.0 release verifies 32 English verse records for the manuscript, identifies the omitted f. 23r marginal quatrain, locates ‘If thou flee idleness’ on f. 33v, and supplies form, incipit, and subject evidence for the songs.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Explains that item-specific terms govern, that most images use CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution, and that commercial reuse requires permission and may incur a fee.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 5665.
evidence.locator: whole manuscript; Padelford targets historical ff. 4b, 28b, 31b, 42b
evidence.summary: Confirms the 149-folio English/Latin music manuscript, its church services, hymns, carols and songs, broad fifteenth- to sixteenth-century dating, onsite access, and absence of exposed Digitised Content.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digital Bodleian object and IIIF manifest for MS. Eng. poet. e. 1.
evidence.locator: single canvas f. 41v; Padelford targets f. 24r and f. 32v not exposed
evidence.summary: Confirms manuscript identity, religious-song/carol contents, c. 1460-1480 date, language, CC BY-NC image terms, and the one-slide limitation.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian object and IIIF manifest for Balliol College MS 354.
evidence.summary: Confirms Richard Hill's commonplace-book, its 1501-1533 London compilation, languages, full public facsimile sequence, and Balliol all-rights-reserved reproduction terms.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of cached Balliol College MS 354 target images against Padelford.
evidence.locator: provider p. 444 / cached item 451, current f. 220v; provider p. 447 / cached item 454, current f. 222r
evidence.summary: Confirms the song 3 ‘Man assay’ version at f. 220v and the song 8 ‘Alma redemptoris’ version at f. 222r, reconciling Padelford's a/b foliation to current recto/verso usage.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 2593.
evidence.locator: whole manuscript; Padelford targets historical ff. 25a and 30b
evidence.summary: Confirms the extant fourteenth-/fifteenth-century poetry collection, onsite access, and absence of exposed public page images.

evidence.citation: J. A. Fuller Maitland, ed., English Carols of the Fifteenth Century (London, 1891), partial public scan.
evidence.locator: frontispiece; pp. v-xi; original notation/text pp. 1-27; carols I, II, III, VII, IX, X
evidence.summary: Verifies edition identity, Trinity-roll source, thirteen-item organization, and all six Padelford dependencies. The available file is not a complete edition scan.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, manuscript O.3.58 catalog and IIIF manifest.
evidence.summary: Confirms the fifteenth-century parchment Trinity Carol Roll, thirteen Latin/Middle English musical carols, holding and history, complete public facsimile, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image license.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, ‘Die Gedichte des Franziskaners Jakob Ryman,’ Archiv 89 (1892): 167-338.
evidence.locator: volume 89 pp. 167-338; exact comparison texts pp. 204, 206, 238
evidence.summary: Confirms Zupitza's edition from Cambridge Ee.1.12 and exact texts matching Padelford songs 11, 10, and 4 at pages 204, 206, and 238.

evidence.citation: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 82 (1889), complete volume scan, checked against Padelford's ‘Archiv 82.206’ citation.
evidence.summary: Page 206 contains unrelated ‘Des Ms. Bodl. 779 jüngere Zusatzlegenden’ material. The exact song 10 comparison instead appears in Zupitza's Archiv 89 p. 206; Padelford's song 4 Ee.1.2 citation likewise conflicts with Zupitza's explicit Ee.1.12 source and matching p. 238 text.

evidence.citation: E. K. Chambers and F. Sidgwick, eds., Early English Lyrics (London, 1907), complete scan.
evidence.locator: items LIX, CVII, CXXV, CXXX, CXXXV, CXLII
evidence.summary: Confirms the prior anthology publication of the six songs named by Padelford, with exact mapping to his song numbers 10, 28, 30, 29, 4, and 15.

evidence.citation: Bibliothèque nationale de France catalog record for Early Bodleian Music reprint, documenting the original 1901 two-volume edition.
evidence.summary: Confirms title, editorial responsibility, two-volume structure, and original London 1901 publication; no complete public scan was verified.

open_questions.question: Can an errata notice or surviving Padelford working note independently confirm that ‘Archiv 82.206’ should read volume 89 and ‘Ee.1.2’ should read Ee.1.12?
next_steps: Search Anglia errata and later Padelford or Ryman scholarship for explicit corrections.
Retain both literal citations and the exact-text correction until independent documentary confirmation is found.

open_questions.question: Can the copying date and relationship of the later f. 33v song 31 be narrowed beyond DIMEV and DIAMM's approximate c. 1500 assessment?
next_steps: Compare the f. 33v hand and textual tradition with dated witnesses and current Selden Carol Book scholarship.
Keep Padelford's 1570-1575 view as a historical claim rather than a current date assertion.

open_questions.question: Can complete or target public facsimiles be obtained for Add MS 5665, Sloane MS 2593, the cited leaves of MS. Eng. poet. e. 1, and Cambridge Ee.1.12?
next_steps: Request target or complete imaging from the British Library, Bodleian, and Cambridge University Library using the exact shelfmarks and locators.
Record item-specific reuse terms before redistributing any supplied images.

open_questions.question: What are the individual composition dates, places, authorships, and textual relationships of Padelford's thirty-one heterogeneous songs?
next_steps: Research and collate each DIMEV work record and its collateral witnesses separately.
Do not infer a single composition date or place from the Selden witness's copying context.

open_questions.question: Which manuscript images may be redistributed in project outputs that can support commercial print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Keep all source-cache bundles Git-ignored and publish source URLs, checksums, and locators by default.
Treat Bodleian and Trinity CC BY-NC images as noncommercial and Balliol images as permission-required.
Obtain written permission before using any of those images in commercial outputs.
